series_description:
Reports, correspondence, or other
information required to be sent to the
Office of the Governor (OOG) by statute or
rule that require no action on the part of
OOG.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Follow statute or rule if a
longer retention is required.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission’s draft
report and documentation of the
agency’s implementation of the
commission’s final
recommendations. The final version
of the agency self-evaluation report
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of
agency item number 98. Does not
include employee complaints; SEE
item number 151.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency.
correspondence, in any format.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include general
correspondence sent or received by
the Governor; See agency item
number 80.

series_title:
Calendars, Appointment
and Itinerary Records -
Executive
series_description:
Calendars, appointment books, and
scheduling/itinerary records, maintained
by staff during business hours, that
document the activities of elected officials,
executive administration, board or
commission members, and division
directors. Includes, but is not limited to,
the Governor's daily schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
agency heads, and board or
commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_title:
Calendars, Appointment
and Itinerary Records -
General
series_description:
Calendars, appointment books, and
scheduling/itinerary records, maintained
by staff during business hours, that
document the activities of agency
employees. Also includes common
calendars used for coordinating work
schedules/assignments or reserving
conference rooms.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number 84a for
calendars of directors, board
members, and executive
administration.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description:
News, press releases, collateral, or any
public relations files maintained or issued
by an agency. Includes print, electronic,
audio, and audiovisual records. records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 102 (Transitory
Information) for images that are no
longer deemed necessary or are
unusable (as extraneous, unusable,
almost-duplicates, etc.).

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of the agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
agency need to be transferred to
the State Archives. For
division/department level
organization charts, see item
number 89b.

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of a division and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from
customers, clients, or stakeholders of an
agency, and the statistical data maintained
rating an agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 112 (Reports and
Studies (Non-Fiscal)) for summary
reports compiled from customer
surveys.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non-personnel
individuals it regulates or serves, including
but not limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
course outlines, and similar training aids
used in external training programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include Crime
Stoppers training; SEE item number
5d. SEE item number 185 for
internal training materials.
SEE ALSO item number 63 (Event
Administration Records).

series_description:
Operational strategic plans prepared in
accordance with Government Code §
481.172(a)(8) (Tourism) or § 2308.104
(TWIC). Also includes the long-range
strategic plan authorized by Government
Code § 2056.009, as applicable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self-evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 101b for ADA
Accommodation Requests.

series_title:
ADA (Americans with
Disabilities Act)
Accommodation Requests
series_description:
Employee/applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the
ADA.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: Intended for meeting
summaries, substantive notes or
reports and supporting
documentation from internal
meetings. Individual reminder notes
or reference notes made by
individuals may possibly be
transitory or reference material.
SEE item number 102.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal), Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing non-
fiscal reports.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 109 (Agency
Performance Measures
Documentation).

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.
Includes reports required by
Education Code § 62.168;
Government Code §§ 436.103,
481.005(d), 481.0066(d-2),
481.00681(h), 481.079, 481.412,
485A.053, 489.107, 490G.007,
772.006(a)(9), 772.0062(d)(6),
772.007(d), 2303.052,
2308.101(a)(14), 2308.101(b),
2308.1015(b), 2308.1016(b),
2310.052; and Human Resources
Code § 115.009(3).

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to the
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 109 (Agency
Performance Measures
Documentation) for supporting
documentation.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: See item number 109
(Agency Performance Measures
Documentation) if the reports are
used to document performance
measures.

series_description:
Information in any format that is publicly
distributed by the agency, statutorily
required to be distributed by the agency,
or produced by the authority of or at the
expense of the agency. The term does not
include information distributed solely to
internal staff, staff of other government
agencies, or contractors/grantees of the
agency. Some examples of non-archival
publications include brochures,
announcements, artwork, and internal
newsletters.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.8).
legal_citation:
Publications
Depository rules are
outlined in 13 TAC §§
3.1-.16

series_description:
Lists of mailing addresses, telephone/fax
numbers, or e-mail addresses maintained
by an agency on its employees or on
entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
NOTE: Records of this nature,
particularly those in databases or
other information systems, may be
overwritten; disposal does not need
to be documented through records
disposition logs.

series_description:
Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_description:
Any record created by the agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service (USPS) or by private
couriers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include Indigov
database; see item numbers 130
(Databases and Information
Systems) and 80 (Executive
Correspondence).
SEE ALSO item number 231
(Directories).

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Projects conducted by agency staff whose
records do not fit under another record
series grouping. Includes notes, reference
materials, work papers, meeting minutes,
correspondence related to the project, and
any other supporting documentation
needed throughout the duration of the
project.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months

series_description:
Records or forms designed and used solely
for data input and control.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_title:
Requisitions for In-Agency
or Inter-Agency
Copy/Printing Service
series_description:
Includes word processing and data
processing.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Formal agreements between the OOG and
other entities to share resources for
mutual benefit. May include Memoranda
of Understanding (MOU), Memoranda of
Agreement (MOA), licensing agreements,
and location agreements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
contracts listed under item number
229 or grant agreements (SEE
Section 4.6.1). Also does not
include ceremonial MOUs signed by
the Governor (SEE item number 83,
Executive Orders).

series_description:
Content (messages, posts, photographs,
videos, etc.) created or received using a
social media application.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Contact the RMO if an
account is to be deactivated or
deleted. Social media content must
remain accessible until retention is
met.

series_title:
Audiovisual Recordings or
Certified Agendas - Closed
Meetings
series_description:
Certified agendas or audiovisual recordings
of closed meetings (executive sessions) of
boards, commissions, committees,
councils, or task forces.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
legal_citation:
Government Code §
551.104(a) for state
boards, commissions,
and other entities
considered
governmental bodies
under the Texas
Open Meetings Act.

series_title:
Meeting Notes - State
Boards, Commissions, and
Councils
series_description:
Notes or transcriptions taken during
meetings of boards, commissions,
committees, councils, or task forces from
which written minutes are prepared. May
include notes taken at advisory committee
meetings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 3 months
comments:
SEE item number 108 for staff
meeting minutes or notes.

series_description:
Meeting agendas, minutes, and board
packets/supporting documentation of
committees and boards that are strictly
advisory and do not control or supervise
public business or policy. Include, but are
not limited to, records of the Aerospace
and Aviation Advisory Committee, Crime
Stoppers Council, Governor’s Commission
for Women, Governor’s University
Research Initiative (GURI) Advisory Board,
Office of Small Business Assistance
Advisory Task Force, Specialty Courts
Advisory Council, Star of Texas Awards
Advisory Committees, and Texas Military
Value Task Force.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 103 for
agendas/minutes of committees
required to conduct meetings in
accordance with Government Code,
Chapter 551; item number 105 for
audiovisual recordings; and item
number 325 for records of
nonprofit boards.
NOTE: In the event of the
abolishment of the Texas Military
Value Task Force, task force records
should be transferred to the Texas
Military Preparedness Commission
in accordance with § 436.1051,
Government Code.

series_description:
Witness registration forms, sign-in sheets,
or similar documents filled out by
members of the public or other state
agencies requesting to speak at an open
meeting or budget hearing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: If all information from the
sign-in sheet is documented in
written minutes of the meeting, the
sign-in sheet need only be retained
for the retention period listed in
item number 106 (Meeting Notes).

series_description:
Bylaws, articles of incorporation, tax-
exempt status recognition documents,
board meeting agendas and minutes,
annual financial statements, and similar
records documenting the history,
structure, and governance of nonprofit
boards affiliated with the Office of the
Governor. Includes records of the Beacon
State Fund, State Agency Council, Texas
Disaster Relief Fund, Texas Governor’s
Mansion Administration (TGMA), Inc., and
Texas Governor's Mansion Greenhouse
Project.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the
records of state boards,
commissions, committees, councils,
task forces, or advisory committees.
SEE items 103, 104, 105, 106, 107,
299, and 321.

series_description:
Applications for gubernatorial
appointments to state agency governing
boards, councils, task forces, and
commissions. Includes records such as
application forms, resumes,
questionnaires, letters of
recommendation, and disclosure forms.
Includes files of both appointed and non-
appointed individuals, but see Archives
Note.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Files for non-
appointed individuals are not
archival.

series_description:
This series documents requests from
individuals or organizations for the
Governor or First Lady to attend an event
or meeting. May include, but is not limited
to: invitations (with their enclosures or
attachments, as applicable),
correspondence, and a copy of the
Governor's daily schedule. Includes both
accepted and declined invitations, but see
Archives Note.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: Invitations received by office
staff may be classified under item
number 81 (General
Correspondence) or 102 (Transitory
Information). ARCHIVES NOTE:
Declined invitations are not
archival.

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE ALSO item number 333
(Proclamation Files).

series_description:
Text of speeches, papers, presentations, or
reports delivered by the Governor,
executive staff, board or commission
members, or division directors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Text of speeches, papers, presentations, or
reports delivered in conjunction with
agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal
documents requiring recipient to appear in
court to testify, or to produce records to
be used in litigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation
in which the state agency is a party,
see item number 98 (Litigation
Files).

series_description:
May include copies of proposed legislation
and related correspondence or analyses.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Log listing all gifts sent directly to the
Office of the Governor or not associated
with the GGRD (Gift, Grant,
Reimbursement, or Donation) form
approval process. Gifts are managed in
accordance with Government Code §
401.101-102.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include GGRD
forms or any logs related to the
GGRD process. SEE item number
180a.

series_description:
Weekly reports, prepared by staff of the
Office of State-Federal Relations (OSFR),
providing legislative updates and
information on division activities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Request/planning forms and related
records documenting state agency
functions held at the Governor’s Mansion.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Copies of financial
records retained for convenience in
the Mansion's event file (e.g.,
invoices, receipts, etc.) must be
discarded prior to transfer to the
State Archives. Financial Services is
the custodian of financial records
and will retain such records
according to the applicable records
series.

series_description:
Files relating to the issuance of
proclamations, which include a copy of the
proclamation and any correspondence
relating to the proclamation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Biennial appropriations budget prepared in
accordance with Government Code §
401.0445 and 401.046.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years and 0 months

series_description:
Records documenting the transition from
an incumbent Governor to the Governor-
elect. Includes briefing materials
summarizing the history, structure,
operations, and information systems of
each division.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Records pertaining to the extradition of
prisoners to or from another state within
the United States. Includes applications to
Governor for issuance of requisition,
Governor's Requisition forms, and
signature authorization memos; may also
include waivers of extradition.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 10 years and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: Filed by date of receipt or
file creation, as applicable.

series_description:
Records pertaining to the transfer of
prisoners to foreign countries under the
provisions of Article IV, Section 11 of the
Constitution of the State of Texas. Includes
applications, letters granting/denying
transfer, copies of judgments, and copies
of constituent communications.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years and 0 months

series_description:
Files typically include Execution Memos
prepared by General Counsel; copies of
court documents (orders, memoranda,
addenda, etc.); correspondence with the
Texas Department of Criminal Justice and
Office of the Attorney General; copies of
constituent communications in support of
or opposition to the execution; and inmate
last statements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Files pertaining to applications for
clemency for prisoners or convicted felons
out on parole seeking a pardon, asking to
have a sentence commuted, or asking for
an emergency medical reprieve. Includes
individual offenders' files and
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Misdemeanor
case files are not archival and may
be destroyed when they meet
retention.

series_description:
Trademark records which pertain to
trademarks registered on behalf of the
agency. May include but not limited to a
copy of the trademark itself as submitted
to the United States Patent and Trademark
Office and the corresponding trademark
registration application, filings, notices,
and supplementary documents.
retention_statement: Permanent plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: Third-party briefs, which
document the agency's actions on
Public Information Act requests
received by other state agencies,
may be classified under item
number 81 (General
Correspondence). Withdrawn
requests may also be classified
under item number 81. Responsive
documents received from 3rd party
agencies are not OOG records and
should be discarded when no longer
administratively valuable.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code), including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit. Includes
settlement packets.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the State
Archives for archival preservation.
legal_citation:
Settlement packets
are maintained
pursuant to Civil
Practices and
Remedies Code §
101.105.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 263 (Visitor
Control Registers) for visitor sign-in
sheets.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code, 552.010.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months

series_description:
Forms granting or obtaining consent for
use of photographs or video footage.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years and 0 months

series_description:
Requests for the Governor's granting of
suspensions of certain statutes and rules
after a disaster. Includes correspondence,
copies of proclamations, and the
suspension list prepared in accordance
with Government Code § 418.0155.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Structured sets of related data organized
for rapid search and retrieval.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: All metadata required to
understand the structure of
database records must be
maintained until the retention
period of the data has been met.

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc. Also includes
Access design objects (forms and reports),
.Net code, and SQL Server Reporting
Services (SSRS) reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.
SEE item number 133 (Technical
Documentation) for user manuals
and similar records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware
documentation needed for access
to electronic records must be
retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition. Includes
documentation describing how a system
operates and which is necessary for using
the system such as: * user guides, system
or sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, and system flowcharts; *
program descriptions and documentation
such as program flowcharts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, and
other records that document modifications
to computer programs; and * data
documentation necessary to access,
retrieve, manipulate and interpret data in
an automated system such as a data
element dictionary, file layout, code book
or table, and other records that explain the
meaning, purpose, structure, logical
relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Technical documentation
needed to access or understand
electronic records (e.g., user
manuals or codebooks) must be
retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Project management records, design
documentation, feasibility studies,
justifications, user requirements, etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Forms created for security purposes to
control or monitor individual access to a
computer/network system and its data.
Internal forms include, but are not limited
to, Security Control (SC) forms, Network
Security Rights (NSR) forms, and Social
Technology Access Forms. External forms
include, but are not limited to, user
agreement forms submitted to the
Comptroller of Public Accounts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months

series_description:
Tickets or work orders for
computer/technical support submitted to
Information Services Division (ISD) and/or
Support Services Division staff.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years and 0 months

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions. May also include
associated design or design modification
requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e., form
SLR 105) that identifies the records that
are created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite or offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include record
survey worksheets used for
retention schedule development;
SEE item number 289.

series_description:
Lists, indexes, finding aids, registers, and
similar documents, in any format, used to
locate and retrieve records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
File plans and similar records that establish
the policies and procedures under which
records and information are managed in
an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113)
or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff to
access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months

series_description:
Includes record survey forms, internal and
external correspondence, research files,
notes, database content, and all other
materials supporting revisions to the
records retention schedule. Also includes
schedule amendments (SLR 122), but see
caution note.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Records Management
Officer must incorporate
amendments to the records
retention schedule (SLR 105) upon
receiving notification of TSLAC
approval.

series_description:
Includes Records Management
Coordinator (RMC) Designation forms and
agency copy of the Records Management
Officer (RMO) Designation form (SLR 104).
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
complaints regarding personnel policies,
working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 98 (Litigation Files).

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years and 0 months

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Resumes or applications received by a

that the resume will be kept on file should
future job openings occur.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 290 (Job Files) for
resumes that are used in any way in
the employment selection process.
Human Resources does not accept
or retain unsolicited resumes.

series_description:
Personnel records maintained on each

evaluations, timesheets, training and
educational achievement records, and
related information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months

series_description:
Internship applications, resumes, outside

records for applicants who are not
selected.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months

series_description:
Journals, notes, correspondence, kudos, or
other documentation maintained on an
individual employee by a supervisor. May
also include telework requests,
agreements, and associated
documentation. Retention period applies
only to records not transferred to Human
Resources for inclusion in the Employee
File.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months
comments:
NOTE: Does not include
performance appraisals; SEE item
number 291 (Employee Files).

series_description:
Includes W-2, 1095-B, 1099, FICA, and
other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years and 0 months

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned

retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 4 years and 0 months

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This

payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years and 0 months
comments:
Human Resources is the custodian
of payroll vouchers.

series_description:
Agency staffing reports (any reports
compiled by the agency on aspects of

staff by program or name; staff hired

employees within the OOG organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of

Information System (HRIS) reports (EEO

reports. May also include supporting
documentation used to create reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years and 0 months
comments:
Financial Services is the custodian
of TWC reports.
legal_citation:
29 CFR § 1602.32 for
EEO reports and
supporting
documentation.

series_description:
Diversity and recruitment plans and
related workforce analyses.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year and 0 months

series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment; includes name, social
security number, exact dates of
employment, last known address, and
most recent public access election
information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years and 0 months
comments: VITAL RECORD

series_title:
Requests/Authorizations
for Training or Other
Reimbursable Activity
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

Gift, Grant,
Reimbursement, or
Donation (GGRD) Forms
series_description:
Requests for approval to receive a gift,
grant, reimbursement, or donation in

401.101. Also includes any logs or other

retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the Gift

item number 295.

series_description:
Aptitude or skills tests required of job

CAUTION: One copy of each

Administration Records -

series_description:
Records documenting the planning,

registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar

programs.

comments:
For training presented to external
entities, SEE item number 97
(Training Materials – External) and
63 (Event Administration Records).

series_description:
Records documenting individual

comments:
SEE item 292 (Intern Files) for intern
timesheets.

series_description:

(Benefits/Payroll Files) for
employee direct deposit
authorizations.

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing

(Banking Records) for other banking

series_description:

60a), and related records. Includes

Administration Files) for purchase

Expenses to Claim memos or Travel

series_description:
Includes budget transfers/revisions (A

K documents).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months and 0 days

series_description: Warrant registers/USAS payable reports.

series_description: Revenue, expenditure, cash, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months and 0 days

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years and 0 months
comments:
VITAL RECORD. SEE ALSO item
numbers 256a, b, and c (Bid
Documentation).

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
VITAL RECORD. SEE ALSO item
numbers 256a, b, and c (Bid
Documentation).

series_description:
Includes successful and unsuccessful bids,
bid requisitions or authorizations, requests
for proposal (RFP), invitations to bid or
propose, bid specifications, and bid
tabulation/evaluation documentation.
Applies to bids associated with a contract
executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 256c for bids that
are withdrawn or not included in
the bid evaluation process.

series_description:
Includes successful and unsuccessful bids,
bid requisitions or authorizations, requests
for proposal (RFP), invitations to bid or
propose, bid specifications, and bid
tabulation/evaluation documentation.
Applies to bids associated with a contract
executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
SEE item number 256c for bids that
are withdrawn or not included in
the bid evaluation process.

series_description:
Unsuccessful bids that are withdrawn or
not included in the bid evaluation process
(missed deadline, incomplete submission,
etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years and 0 months

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of

and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_description:
Requests for information or quotes
preliminary to the procurement of goods
or services by direct purchase or bid.

comments:
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to a request for
proposal (RFP) or bid, the RFI
documentation must be retained in
accordance with item number 256a,
256b, or 256c, as applicable.

series_description:
List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements including general obligation,

contracts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriation requests by the
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years and 0 months
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Copies are sent to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program at the time of
submission to the Legislative
Budget Board (LBB). Working files
and related documentation used in
creating the final plan are not
subject to archival review and may
be disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_title:
Fiscal Management
Reports and Working
Papers
series_description:
Includes internal operating budget, budget
reports and analyses, cash balance reports,

Review Board reports, fiscal notes, or
other fiscal reports or internal budget
requests. Also includes working papers for
such reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the

SEE item number 216.

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers and
revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
For internal operating budgets,
including budget working papers
created at the division/program
level, SEE item number 213
(Internal Fiscal Management
Reports).
ARCHIVES NOTE: Archival
requirement is met by sending
copies to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
TSLAC.

series_description:
Daily USAS reports generated or received

Report) 2021, 3331, 3601, and 8200.
Monthly USAS reports generated by the
agency. Includes, but is not limited to,
DAFR 5651, 5801, 5851, 7400, 7470, 7500,
and 8660.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 0 years and 0 months
comments:

(Expenditure Journals or Registers)
for DAFR 3691, Direct Deposit
Payees by Agency.

series_description:
Yearly report complied from the
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

series_description:
Applications, grant authorization records,
review and monitoring documentation,
disbursement documentation, and other
information related to Texas Enterprise
Fund awards made by the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months

series_description:
Records relating to the creation of
programs and funding for emerging
technology industries. Includes application,
review and compliance documents, and
RCIC (Regional Center for Innovation &
Commercialization) contracts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE

(Purchase/Payment Vouchers).
Program abolished as of September
1, 2015. Senate Bill 632, 84(R).

series_description:
Applications, grant authorization records,
grant/project financial or performance
reports, review and monitoring
documentation, and other information
related to federally-funded grants or
awards made or received by the agency.
Includes criminal justice, homeland
security, and Wagner-Peyser grants.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months

may vary depending on the specific

CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

series_description:
Applications, grant authorization records,
grant/project financial or performance
reports, review and monitoring
documentation, and other information
related to state-funded grants or awards
made or received by the agency. Includes
state-funded criminal justice grants,
homeland security grants, Texas Moving
Image Industry Incentive Program (TMIIIP),
Defense Economic Adjustment Assistance
Grant (DEAAG), Governor's University
Research Initiative (GURI), Spaceport Trust

other state grant except where noted
below. CAUTION: Does not include Texas
Enterprise Fund (SEE item number 60a),
Texas Disaster Fund (SEE item number
218c), or Emerging Technology Fund (see
item number 62a).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months

CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

Awarded/Funded

Request letters, grant authorization
records, review and monitoring
documentation, and other information
related to Texas Disaster Fund awards
made by the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years and 0 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

series_description:
Unfunded applications for grant, award, or
incentive programs (federal, state, or
other). Includes evaluation documentation

series_description:

comments:
NOTE: The retention period starts
from the end of the fiscal year
covered by the plan.

series_description:

series_description:
Records documenting financial obligations

obligations.

CAUTION: Does not include

Administration Files). Does not

Industrial Revenue Bonds; SEE item
number 34a.
legal_citation:
Texas Constitution
art. III, §§ 49-n, 52-a;
art. XVI, § 71. 26 CFR
§ 1.148-5(d)(6)(iii)(E)

series_description:

Files used to administer state entities'

Reimbursement Program, Motor Sports
Racing Trust Fund, and Events Trust Fund.
Documentation includes application
documents and disbursement documents
(requests, receipts, contracts) from
applying entities (cities, counties,
municipalities, organizations, other)
related to an event eligible for the
program. Documentation also includes
funds management documentation
including worksheets and event history
that are associated with state and local
participation.
